agent_output:
# ML-Based Delivery Time Estimation System вҖ” Architecture

## Executive Summary

This system requires a lean, latency-sensitive ML serving architecture with a robust offline training pipeline and safe model rollback. Given the 4-person team and 100ms P99 constraint, I recommend a **two-plane architecture**: a fast synchronous inference plane (pre-loaded model, no network hops to model servers) and an asynchronous training plane that produces versioned model artifacts. Model serving uses a sidecar/embedded approach (not a separate model microservice) to eliminate network latency, and a feature store pre-computes expensive features to keep inference fast.

---

## Architecture

### Components

| # | Component | Responsibility | Boundaries |
|---|-----------|---------------|------------|
| 1 | **Feature Store** | Pre-computes and serves features (courier load, zone stats, weather cache). Separates feature logic from model logic. | Owns feature engineering and storage. Does NOT own raw order data or model artifacts. |
| 2 | **Inference Service** | Loads model artifact into memory, accepts prediction requests, returns estimated delivery time. Runs as part of the checkout API (embedded or sidecar). | Owns model loading, input validation, prediction. Does NOT own training, feature computation, or model selection. |
| 3 | **Model Registry** | Stores versioned model artifacts with metadata (metrics, training date, dataset hash). Source of truth for which model version is "active". | Owns model versioning, promotion, and rollback state. Does NOT own training or serving. |
| 4 | **Training Pipeline** | Weekly batch job: pulls delivery data, engineers features, trains model, evaluates against holdout set, registers new artifact. | Owns training orchestration, hyperparameter tuning, evaluation. Does NOT own serving or promotion decisions. |
| 5 | **Model Gateway / Switcher** | Thin control plane that tells Inference Service which model version to load. Handles rollback commands. | Owns promotion/rollback signaling. Does NOT own the artifacts themselves. |
| 6 | **Monitoring & Alerting** | Tracks prediction accuracy (predicted vs. actual delivery time), latency percentiles, feature drift, model drift. | Owns observability. Does NOT own remediation (humans decide rollback). |

### Interfaces

| From | To | Contract | Coupling |
|------|----|----------|----------|
| Checkout API | Inference Service | `POST /predict` вҶ’ `{ order_weight, distance_km, zone_id, hour_of_day, weather_code, courier_load }` вҶ’ `{ estimated_minutes, confidence_interval, model_version }` | **Sync, tight** (latency-critical path) |
| Inference Service | Feature Store | `GET /features/realtime?zone=X&courier=Y` вҶ’ `{ courier_load_avg, zone_delay_p50, weather_severity }` | **Sync, moderate** (cached, sub-10ms) |
| Training Pipeline | Feature Store | Batch read of historical features | **Async, loose** (batch job) |
| Training Pipeline | Model Registry | `PUT /models/{version}` with artifact + metrics | **Async, loose** |
| Model Gateway | Inference Service | Config push or poll: `{ active_model_version: "v42" }` | **Async, loose** (config-driven) |
| Model Gateway | Model Registry | `GET /models/active`, `POST /models/{version}/promote`, `POST /models/{version}/rollback` | **Sync, moderate** |
| Monitoring | Inference Service | Scrape metrics endpoint `/metrics` (Prometheus) | **Async, loose** |
| Monitoring | Model Gateway | Alert webhook вҶ’ triggers rollback review | **Async, loose** |

### Data Flow

```
[Checkout Request]
       в”Ӯ
       в–ј
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ    sub-10ms     в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ Checkout API в”Ӯв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–¶в”Ӯ Feature Store  в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ              в”Ӯв—Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ӯ (Redis/cache)  в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ              в”Ӯ                 в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

в”Ӯ              в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ              в–ј
в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ Inference Service  в”Ӯ в—Җв”Җв”Җ model artifact loaded in-process
в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ (embedded model)   в”Ӯ     from Model Registry at startup
в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ     + hot-reload on version change
в”Ӯ              в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ              в–ј
в”Ӯ     { estimated_minutes: 34,
в”Ӯ       confidence: [28, 41],      в—Җв”Җв”Җ P99 < 100ms total
в”Ӯ       model_version: "v42" }
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ

OFFLINE (weekly):

в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ    в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ    в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ

в”Ӯ (historical) в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ Pipeline      в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ (S3 + metadata)в”Ӯ

                   в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ          в”Ӯ
                          в”Ӯ                   в–ј
                   Evaluate against      в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
                   holdout set           в”Ӯ Gateway  в”Ӯ
                   в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–¶   в”Ӯ (promote)в”Ӯ
                   metrics pass?         в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

### Patterns Used

| Pattern | Rationale |
|---------|-----------|
| **Embedded Model (Sidecar)** | Eliminates network hop to a model server. For a single lightweight model (gradient boosted trees or small NN), loading into the inference service process keeps P99 well under 100ms. A 4-person team can't afford to operate a separate model serving cluster. |
| **Feature Store (dual-mode)** | Batch features for training, real-time features for serving. Prevents training/serving skew вҖ” the #1 cause of ML production bugs. Redis for online, Parquet/warehouse for offline. |
| **Model Registry with Immutable Artifacts** | Every model version is an immutable artifact with metadata. Enables instant rollback by pointer swap, not retraining. |
| **Blue/Green Model Deployment** | Two model slots in the inference service. New model loads into the inactive slot; traffic switches atomically. Rollback = switch back. Zero-downtime model updates. |
| **Shadow Scoring** | New model scores in shadow mode alongside active model before promotion. Catches regressions before they affect users. |
| **Circuit Breaker on Prediction** | If model inference fails, fall back to a simple heuristic (distance-based lookup table). Checkout never blocks on ML failure. |

---

## Domain Model

### Bounded Contexts

| Context | Classification | Aggregates |
|---------|---------------|------------|
| **Prediction Serving** | **Core** вҖ” this is the competitive advantage, the customer-facing estimation | `PredictionRequest`, `PredictionResult`, `ModelArtifact` (loaded) |
| **Model Lifecycle** | **Core** вҖ” reliability of model updates and rollback is business-critical | `ModelVersion`, `PromotionPolicy`, `RollbackEvent` |
| **Feature Engineering** | **Supporting** вҖ” necessary but largely mechanical; transforms raw data into features | `FeatureDefinition`, `FeatureVector`, `FeatureSnapshot` |
| **Training Orchestration** | **Supporting** вҖ” scheduled batch job, important but not differentiating | `TrainingRun`, `EvaluationReport`, `Dataset` |
| **Delivery Data** | **Generic** вҖ” standard transactional data from the logistics platform | `DeliveryRecord`, `CourierAssignment` |

### Context Map

| Upstream | Downstream | Relationship |
|----------|-----------|-------------|
| Delivery Data | Training Orchestration | **Conformist** вҖ” training consumes delivery data as-is |
| Feature Engineering | Prediction Serving | **Shared Kernel** вҖ” feature schema is shared to prevent training/serving skew |
| Feature Engineering | Training Orchestration | **Shared Kernel** вҖ” same feature definitions |
| Training Orchestration | Model Lifecycle | **Customer-Supplier** вҖ” training produces artifacts, lifecycle decides promotion |
| Model Lifecycle | Prediction Serving | **Open Host Service** вҖ” serving reads active model version via a clean API |

---

## Trade-offs

| Decision | Gains | Sacrifices | Alternatives Considered |
|----------|-------|------------|------------------------|
| **Embedded model (not separate model server like TF Serving/Triton)** | Sub-10ms inference, no network hop, simple ops for 4-person team | Can't scale model serving independently from API; limits model to what fits in process memory | TF Serving/Triton (rejected: operational overhead for small team, unnecessary for single lightweight model), SageMaker endpoints (rejected: adds 10-30ms network latency) |
| **Gradient Boosted Trees (XGBoost/LightGBM) over deep learning** | Fast inference (~1ms), interpretable, small artifact (~10MB), trains in minutes not hours | Less capacity for complex non-linear patterns (e.g., traffic congestion sequences) | Deep neural network (rejected: slower inference, harder to debug, overkill for tabular features), Linear regression (rejected: too simple, misses feature interactions) |
| **Redis feature store over custom solution** | Battle-tested, sub-ms lookups, team likely already has Redis | Another infrastructure component to maintain | In-process feature cache (rejected: harder to share between training/serving), Feast (rejected: heavy for 4-person team, adds operational burden) |
| **Weekly batch retraining over online/continuous learning** | Simple, debuggable, easy to validate before promotion | Model doesn't adapt to sudden shifts (e.g., new road closures) until next week | Online learning (rejected: hard to debug, risk of silent degradation), Daily retraining (considered viable if weekly proves too stale) |
| **Shadow scoring before promotion over A/B testing** | Safe validation without splitting user traffic; simpler implementation | No real-world A/B comparison with user behavior changes | A/B testing (valid but complex for 4 people), Canary deployment (good middle ground if shadow scoring proves insufficient) |

---

## Flexibility Points

| Area | Evolution Path |
|------|---------------|
| **Model type** | Artifact is opaque to the serving layer вҖ” can swap XGBoost вҶ’ neural network вҶ’ ensemble without changing the serving infrastructure. Just produce a new artifact that implements the `predict(features) вҶ’ minutes` interface. |
| **Feature set** | Feature store is schema-versioned. Add new features by: (1) add to store, (2) retrain with new feature, (3) update serving feature fetch. Old models still work with old feature schema via versioning. |
| **Retraining frequency** | Pipeline is parameterized by schedule. Change weekly вҶ’ daily вҶ’ hourly by adjusting the cron trigger. No code changes. |
| **Serving topology** | Can graduate from embedded model to dedicated model server (Triton, TF Serving) when team grows or model complexity demands it. Interface stays the same. |

### Rigidity Points (Intentional)

| Area | Reason |
|------|--------|
| **Feature parity between training and serving** | Training/serving skew is the #1 ML production bug. The shared feature store and shared feature definitions are intentionally rigid вҖ” you MUST use the same feature pipeline for both. |
| **Immutable model artifacts** | Once registered, a model version is never mutated. This is non-negotiable for rollback safety and audit. |
| **Fallback heuristic always present** | The distance-based fallback must always exist and be tested. ML is probabilistic; the system must degrade gracefully. |
| **Model promotion requires evaluation gate** | No model goes live without passing the holdout evaluation. Manual override exists but is logged and alerted. |

---

## Risks

| Risk | Severity | Mitigation |
|------|----------|------------|
| **Training/serving skew** вҖ” features computed differently in batch vs. real-time | **Critical** | Shared feature store with identical transformation code. Integration tests that compare batch vs. real-time feature output for the same input. |
| **P99 latency breach during model hot-reload** | **High** | Blue/green model slots. New model loads into inactive slot; pointer swap is atomic. Requests in-flight continue on old model. |
| **Model degradation not detected** | **High** | Monitor predicted vs. actual delivery time (delayed feedback loop). Alert when MAE exceeds threshold over rolling window. Auto-trigger rollback review. |
| **Feature store (Redis) goes down** | **High** | Local feature cache with 60s TTL as L1. If Redis is unavailable, serve from cache. If cache is cold, fall back to heuristic. Circuit breaker pattern. |
| **Data quality regression in delivery data** | **Medium** | Data validation checks in the training pipeline (Great Expectations or similar). Training aborts if data quality checks fail. |
| **4-person team stretched too thin** | **Medium** | Use managed services where possible (managed Redis, S3 for registry, Airflow Cloud or simple cron). Avoid building custom ML infrastructure. The architecture is deliberately simple. |
| **Concept drift вҖ” delivery patterns change (new zones, COVID-like events)** | **Medium** | Monitoring detects drift via prediction error trends. Can increase retraining frequency. Feature store allows adding contextual features (holidays, events). |

---

## Implementation Guidance

### Recommended Approach

**Phase 1 (Weeks 1-3): Serving path first.** Get the prediction endpoint live with a hand-tuned heuristic model, then swap in the ML model. This validates the latency requirements and integration with checkout before ML complexity is added.

**Phase 2 (Weeks 3-5): Training pipeline.** Build the batch training pipeline, feature store (batch mode), model registry. Train V1 model, validate offline metrics.

**Phase 3 (Weeks 5-7): Connect the loop.** Wire model registry вҶ’ inference service hot-reload. Add shadow scoring. Add monitoring. Deploy V1 model to production.

**Phase 4 (Ongoing): Iterate.** Improve features, tune model, increase retraining frequency as needed.

### Team Allocation

| Engineer | Focus |
|----------|-------|
| ML Engineer 1 | Feature engineering, model training, evaluation metrics |
| ML Engineer 2 | Feature store (batch + real-time), data quality checks |
| Platform Engineer 1 | Inference service, model loading, latency optimization, fallback |
| Platform Engineer 2 | Model registry, promotion/rollback gateway, CI/CD, monitoring |

### Files / Components to Create

```
services/
  prediction-service/          # Inference service (Go or Rust for latency, or Python with C extension)
    src/
      server.py                # HTTP handler, feature assembly, prediction
      model_loader.py          # Blue/green model loading, hot-reload
      feature_client.py        # Feature store client with caching + fallback
      fallback.py              # Heuristic fallback (distance/zone lookup table)
    tests/
      test_latency.py          # Load test asserting P99 < 100ms
      test_fallback.py         # Verify fallback activates on model failure
      test_feature_parity.py   # Compare batch vs real-time features

  feature-store/               # Feature engineering
    src/
      definitions.py           # Shared feature definitions (training + serving)
      batch_compute.py         # Batch feature computation for training
      realtime_compute.py      # Real-time feature updates (courier load, weather)
    tests/
      test_parity.py           # Batch vs realtime output comparison

  training-pipeline/           # Weekly training job
    src/
      train.py                 # Model training (XGBoost/LightGBM)
      evaluate.py              # Holdout evaluation, comparison to current model
      register.py              # Push artifact to model registry
    config/
      training_config.yaml     # Hyperparameters, schedule, thresholds
    tests/
      test_data_quality.py     # Data validation checks

  model-registry/              # Model versioning (can be S3 + DynamoDB, or MLflow)
    src/
      registry.py              # Store/retrieve/promote/rollback artifacts
      gateway.py               # API for promotion and rollback commands

infra/
  docker-compose.yaml          # Local dev: Redis, prediction service, training
  monitoring/
    dashboards/                # Grafana dashboards for latency, accuracy, drift
    alerts.yaml                # Alert rules for P99, MAE, model staleness
```

### Patterns to Follow

- **Feature definitions as code** вҖ” single source of truth imported by both training and serving
- **Model artifact = pickle/joblib + metadata JSON** вҖ” keep it simple, version in S3
- **Health check endpoint** that verifies model is loaded and feature store is reachable
- **Structured logging** with `model_version`, `prediction_ms`, `features_ms` on every request

### Pitfalls to Avoid

- **DO NOT** call the model server over the network for real-time inference вҖ” embed it
- **DO NOT** compute features differently in training vs. serving вҖ” share the code
- **DO NOT** skip the fallback heuristic вҖ” it's your safety net when ML fails
- **DO NOT** auto-promote models without evaluation gate вҖ” always compare to current model on holdout set
- **DO NOT** build a custom ML platform вҖ” use existing tools (MLflow for registry, Airflow for orchestration, Redis for features)
- **DO NOT** start with a complex model вҖ” XGBoost on tabular features will get you 80% of the way with 20% of the complexity

---

## Confidence Assessment

| | |
|---|---|
| **Confidence Level** | **85%** |
| **High Confidence Areas** | Embedded model serving for latency, feature store for skew prevention, blue/green for rollback, XGBoost as starting model, phased rollout plan |
| **Low Confidence Areas** | Exact latency budget breakdown (need to benchmark feature store round-trip in their infrastructure), whether weekly retraining is frequent enough (depends on delivery pattern volatility), Redis vs. in-process cache trade-off (depends on feature cardinality) |
| **Assumptions** | (1) Model is tabular and fits in ~100MB memory, (2) Feature count is <100, (3) Team has existing Redis and S3 infrastructure, (4) Checkout API is already HTTP-based, (5) Historical delivery data is clean enough to train on with moderate preprocessing, (6) Weather data is available via external API with acceptable latency |
